situation: audit-2026-07-07-030 finding FE-H-001: SSRF via user-controlled LLM base_url persisted to localStorage in AiSettings.tsx. User input flows to chatLoop override.base_url (line 78) and api.updateLlmConfig without scheme/hostname validation. Attackers can inject file://, javascript:, or attacker-controlled https URLs to exfiltrate API keys or trigger backend SSRF.
action: Add shared validateLlmBaseUrl() module enforcing https-only (localhost exception for dev), blocking file:/javascript:/data:/vbscript:/blob: schemes, rejecting embedded userinfo. Wire validator into AiSettings.tsx handleSave + handlePushToServer + Form.Item rules for real-time feedback. Add runtime guard in chatLoop.ts to skip override when localStorage contains stale invalid URL (falls back to server default).
outcome_evidence: Commit db2a4bd on auto/opt-frontend-1783366395 pushed to origin. 3 files changed (+89/-33). Validator rejects file:/javascript:/data: schemes, enforces https (localhost exempt), blocks userinfo. AiSettings shows inline error on invalid URL. chatLoop runtime guard logs warning and skips override for stale localStorage values.
next_suggestion: Dispatch FE-H-002 (prompt injection bypasses medium-risk tool confirm) next — also high severity from audit-030.

audit_findings.finding: handleSave accepted arbitrary base_url without scheme/hostname validation → localStorage persistence of malicious URL
audit_findings.remediation: Call validateLlmBaseUrl() before setConfig; reject with message.error on failure

audit_findings.finding: handlePushToServer forwarded unvalidated base_url to backend /llm/config → SSRF + API key exfil
override.base_url set from localStorage without runtime validation → stale malicious URL triggers SSRF on every chat
audit_findings.remediation: Import validateLlmBaseUrl; skip override when validator rejects, log warning, fall back to server default
